aspects.label: Not honored in their own country
Were called: Holy men of God
God communicated to: His secret things
God communicated to: At various time and in different ways
God communicated to: By an audible voice
God communicated to: By dreams and visions
Were under the influence of the Holy Spirit while prophesying
Spoke in the name of the Lord
Frequently spoke in parables and riddles
Frequently in their actions, &c were made signs to the people
Frequently left without divine communication on account of sins of the people
Were required: To be bold and undaunted
Were required: To be vigilant and faithful
Were required: To receive with attention all God’s communications
Were required: Not to speak anything but what they received from God
Were required: To declare everything that the Lord commanded
Sometimes received divine communications and uttered predictions under great bodily and mental excitement
Sometimes uttered their predictions in verse
Often accompanied by music while predicting
Often committed their predictions to writing
Writings of, read in the synagogues every Sabbath
Ordinary: Trained up and instructed in schools
Ordinary: The sacred bards of the Jews
Extraordinary: Specially raised up on occasions of emergency
Extraordinary: Often endued with miraculous power
Wore a coarse dress of hair-cloth
Often led a wandering and unsettled life
Simple in their manner of life
The historiographers of the Jewish nation
Presented with gifts by those who consulted them
Sometimes thought it right to reject presents
Were sent to: Reprove the wicked and exhort to repentance
Were sent to: Denounce the wickedness of kings
Were sent to: Exhort to faithfulness and constancy in God’s service
Were sent to: Predict the coming of Christ
Were sent to: Predict the downfall of nations
Felt deeply on account of the calamities which they predicted
Predictions of: Frequently proclaimed at the gate of the Lord’s house
Predictions of: Proclaimed in the cities and streets
Predictions of: Written on tables and fixed up in some public place
Predictions of: Written on rolls and read to the people
Assisted the Jews in their great national undertakings
Mentioned in scripture: Prophet set to Israel
Mentioned in scripture: Prophet sent to Eli
Mentioned in scripture: Prophet of Judah
Mentioned in scripture: Azariah the son of Oded
Mentioned in scripture: Jehu the son of Hanani
Mentioned in scripture: Micaiah the son of Imlah
Mentioned in scripture: Zechariah son of Iddo
Mentioned in scripture: Zacharias the father of John
Mentioned in scripture: Daughters of Philip
One generally attached to the king’s household
The Jews: Require to hear and believe
The Jews: Often tried to make them speak smooth things
The Jews: Often put them to death
The Jews: Often left without, on account of sin
God avenged all injuries done to
Christ predicted to exercise the office of
